title: Dispatcher Jobs View
tech: Vite, React 18, TypeScript, React Router v6, Axios, CSS Modules
keyConstraints: TypeScript strict, no UI component library, plain CSS Modules only, Axios instance already configured with auth interceptor
description: Implement the Dispatcher/Admin jobs view. This replaces the JobsPage placeholder with a fully functional page. Dispatchers can see all jobs, create new jobs, assign drivers, and update job status. Uses the existing backend API at /api/jobs. Keep UI simple and functional — no complex design needed.
acceptanceCriteria: JobsPage fetches GET /api/jobs on mount and displays jobs in a list/table showing: title, status, assigned driver name (or Unassigned), scheduledAt
Jobs list shows loading state while fetching and error state if request fails
Create Job button opens a form (inline or simple modal) with fields: title (required), description (optional), scheduledAt (optional), location (optional)
Create job form submits POST /api/jobs, adds new job to list on success, shows error on failure
Each job row has an Assign Driver button that opens a simple dropdown of available drivers fetched from GET /api/users?role=DRIVER
Assigning a driver calls PATCH /api/jobs/:id with assignedDriverId and updates the list
Each job row has a Status button that shows allowed next statuses and calls PATCH /api/jobs/:id with the new status
Status badges are color coded: DRAFT=gray, ASSIGNED=blue, IN_PROGRESS=yellow, COMPLETED=green
All API errors show a simple error message to the user — no raw error objects
securityConsiderations: Never pass user role or id from frontend state to the API as part of the request body — the backend reads these from the JWT. The Assign Driver dropdown must only show users with role DRIVER — enforce this via the API query parameter, not by filtering frontend state.
